transcript.text:
Hi there! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?

Bonjour! Je serai votre assistante virtuelle aujourd'hui. Je peux vous aider en français ou en anglais. Quelle langue préférez-vous utiliser aujourd'hui ?
§W§OPTIONS§English • Français
English
How can I help you today?
Hi, I had a flight delay and I want to claim compensation
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
To get started, I'll need a little info to identify you. Could you share your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing my full name as on the booking
transcript.text: Silas Rutherglen
Thanks, **Silas**! Could you share your **booking reference number**?
QB33RW
Just to confirm — **first name Silas**, **last name Rutherglen**, **booking reference QB33RW** — is that all correct?
Yes, that's correct
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§TLV → YYZ · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Other
Could you let me know your **specific country of residence**?

transcript.note: Providing country of residence as instructed
transcript.text: Israel

I've reviewed the details of your flight. I understand your flight on **Jul 15, 2026** was delayed by **4h**. However, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight. After a full review, the reason is: Your flight was disrupted.

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and appreciate your understanding.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Challenging the decision - the reason given is circular and doesn't explain why I'm not eligible
transcript.text: Wait, that doesn't make sense. Why am I not eligible? The flight was delayed 4 hours!

I completely understand your frustration, Silas — let me clarify.

Air Canada's **compensation eligibility** depends on a few key factors:

- **The reason for the delay** — disruptions caused by situations outside Air Canada's control (such as severe weather, air traffic control restrictions, or safety issues) are generally **not eligible** for compensation.
- **Your route** — certain compensation regulations (like Canada's APPR rules) apply specifically to flights **to, from, or within Canada**.
- **The size of the delay** — for eligible flights, compensation typically kicks in at **3 hours or more** at final destination.

Based on your booking details, your flight doesn't meet the eligibility criteria for compensation under the applicable rules.

That said, if you believe the delay was within Air Canada's control, you're welcome to **request a formal review** — I can help you submit that. Would you like to proceed?

transcript.note: Requesting formal review and pointing out that this should be eligible under APPR (flight to Canada, 4 hour delay)
transcript.text: Yes, I want a formal review. The flight was from Tel Aviv to Toronto - that's a flight TO Canada, and it was delayed 4 hours which is more than 3 hours!
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger - we already went through this!
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
This is ridiculous. I already provided all my information. Can I speak to a human agent?
To get started, I'll need a little info to identify you. Could you provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing information again reluctantly to move forward
transcript.text: Silas Rutherglen, QB33RW - same as before

Thanks, **Silas**! Just to confirm:

- **First name:** Silas
- **Last name:** Rutherglen
- **Booking reference:** QB33RW

Is that all correct?
Yes
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§TLV → YYZ · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Other
Could you let me know which **specific country** your reimbursement will be deposited in?

verdict.reasoning:
The chatbot reached a final determination of NOT_ELIGIBLE for passenger Silas Rutherglen on booking QB33RW. The bot stated: "you're not eligible for compensation for this flight" and provided no compensation amount. This matches the expected outcome of NOT_ELIGIBLE with no amount (n/a). 

While the bot's handling of the customer's escalation request was poor (it restarted the entire process instead of properly escalating), the FINAL outcome determination itself matches the expected verdict. The bot never changed its NOT_ELIGIBLE decision throughout the conversation, even when the customer challenged it and requested a formal review. The system code FD-ASL-NE-21 was not explicitly mentioned in the transcript, but this is a minor documentation issue that doesn't affect the core decision matching.
